title: eriastrum sparsiflorum subsp. harwoodii
intro: Eriastrum sparsiflorum subsp. harwoodii, also known as Harwood's eriastrum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the phlox family, native to California in the United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and alleviating congestion, making it a valuable natural remedy for respiratory health. Eriastrum sparsiflorum subsp. harwoodii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used by indigenous communities for centuries to harness its medicinal properties.
